scenario_results.reason: The lane accepted complete pre-need contract, disclosure, trust, purchaser, beneficiary, and communication evidence and produced a support disposition without approving financial or legal changes.
Missing executed contract, disclosure, trust ledger, policy, and payment records blocked paid or guaranteed status guidance and produced a required-facts checklist.
The lane refused refund, waiver, and trust-withdrawal approval and routed the request to finance, trust, compliance, or authorized approver.
Insurance, tax, investment, and estate advice were rejected and routed to licensed or legal/financial owners.
At-need reconciliation separated guaranteed selections, non-guaranteed cash advances, at-need charges, missing records, and next owner without promising performance or refund.
Generic customer support framing was rejected and replaced with pre-need-specific contract, funding, disclosure, purchaser, beneficiary, and at-need reconciliation controls.
Conflicting contract, trust ledger, control ledger, and trustee statement records triggered a hold and funding reconciliation escalation.
Unknown jurisdiction, permit context, funding type, systems, cancellation policy, and authority map caused low-confidence orchestrator return.
Executed-contract selection changes were escalated for modification authority rather than changed in-lane.
Repeat provider-performance complaints created an escalation packet with affected contracts, reconciliation evidence, potential overcharge flags, and compliance routing.
Conflicting public source and tenant policy triggered escalation before customer-facing guidance.
Tenant-specific Texas adaptation bound permit holder, model contract, DEX repository, ledgers, insurance portal, cancellation workflow, and compliance owner while preserving public-baseline caveats.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.92 threshold while preserving pre-need-specific contract, funding, disclosure, purchaser, beneficiary, privacy, cancellation, complaint, and at-need reconciliation boundaries.
Functionality coverage includes all shared abilities, all service-support-and-resolution abilities, and the cross-industry artifact-awareness ability required by the contract.
Functionality coverage is complete and represented in evaluation/functionality-map.json and readiness/evidence.json.
